fields.description:
# Provision AWS-hosted Gerrit + rebar MCP; lock rebar's GitHub `main` to Gerrit

## Context
We are adopting Gerrit as the gatekeeper for all code merged to rebar's `main`, self-hosted on AWS alongside the rebar MCP server, with GitHub as a downstream read-only mirror (the Go-project topology). This epic stands up that environment and locks the rebar GitHub repo so `main` only advances via commits Gerrit replicates after its submit requirements pass. It is the prerequisite that enables dogfooding the agentic code-review capability on rebar itself.

## Success Criteria
- A single AWS instance hosts Gerrit (NoteDb, no external DB) + the rebar MCP server.
- rebar's GitHub `main` can only be updated by Gerrit's replication identity; all other pushes / PR-merges / force-pushes are rejected.
- An `LLM-Review` label + submit requirement are wired to the rebar MCP server, and a change flows end-to-end (push to Gerrit -> review -> submit -> replicate to GitHub).

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] AWS instance (~8 GB; e.g. t4g.large) provisioned; Gerrit + rebar MCP each containerized; Gerrit heap pinned (~2 GB); reverse proxy on 443; Gerrit HTTP 8080 + SSH 29418.
- [ ] Gerrit rebar project configured: `change.submitWholeTopic=true`; custom `LLM-Review` label; submit requirement = the SINGLE deterministic vote `label:LLM-Review=MAX AND -has:unresolved` (the LLM-Review label is voted by the deterministic pass-3 blocker based on thresholds; there is exactly ONE code-review vote, with no separate human Code-Review or CI Verified votes); `copyCondition` carrying the vote across trivial rebases.
- [ ] Gerrit replication plugin pushes `main` -> GitHub (non-force) as a dedicated `gerrit-replication` GitHub App / service account.
- [ ] GitHub rebar repo locked: ruleset restricts `main` updates to the replication identity only (block force-push + deletions); repo write limited to that identity; no human/admin bypass.
- [ ] rebar MCP server reachable by Gerrit (stream-events/webhook) and can vote `LLM-Review` via REST.
- [ ] End-to-end proof: a test change is reviewed + submitted in Gerrit and appears on GitHub `main`; a direct GitHub push / PR-merge to `main` is rejected.


## Decomposition & resolved design questions (2026-06-28 brainstorm + derisk)
Decomposed into 7 stories S1–S7 (deps: S1<-S2<-S3<-{S4,S5}; S5<-S6; {S4,S5,S6}<-S7). The plan-review gaps on the original ACs are resolved as follows:
- **IaC tooling + remote state** (was unspecified): Terraform with an S3 backend (`use_lockfile`, TF >= 1.10) + a one-time state-bucket bootstrap — S1.
- **Secrets management** (GitHub deploy key, Gerrit REST/bot token, MCP HMAC signing key, ANTHROPIC_API_KEY): AWS SSM Parameter Store SecureString + a least-privilege instance-role policy, pulled to a `chmod 600` `.env` at boot — S1 (scoping reinforced in S4).
- **rebar MCP is stdio-only** → Gerrit cannot reach it directly: S2 makes the MCP HTTP-reachable (streamable-http transport OR a thin in-repo webhook→review→vote shim) behind nginx at `/mcp/`.
- **Gerrit event mechanism** (the unresolved "stream-events/webhook" OR): COMMITTED to the `webhooks` plugin (push) + the `events-log` plugin with a reconciler poller for backfill — S4. (Not SSH stream-events.)
- **NoteDb persistence + backup** (single-box durability): NoteDb on a dedicated gp3 EBS volume at `/var/gerrit` with `prevent_destroy` + DLM daily snapshots (S1) and a backup/restore drill (S7); merged code is always safe on the GitHub mirror.
- **pass3 verdict → `LLM-Review` integer mapping** (was unspecified): explicit 4-outcome fail-closed mapping (passed=+MAX; block/dropped/indeterminate=-1; advisory=0), config-driven — S4.

**AC4 refinement:** the lockdown is a REPO-LEVEL ruleset (navapbc is a `free` org; admin-proof org-level rulesets need a paid plan). So "no human/admin bypass" means no direct/accidental pushes and force-push/deletion blocked; TRUSTED repo admins can deliberately edit/disable the rule — an explicitly accepted PoC risk. The existing `main-protection` ruleset (PR + status-checks) is replaced by the mirror lock (S6).
